projects.title: Illinois Flexible Pavement Design and Monitoring
projects.summary: Current project focused on updating IDOT's pavement design procedures by reviewing 20+ years of extended-life projects, and evaluating emerging factors such as climate change, truck platooning, and increased axle loads. This project encompasses field and laboratory analysis of AC mixes used for perpetual pavement configurations, including fracture and fatigue tests, network-level evaluation using Traffic Speed Deflectometer, characterization of multiscale mechanical responses under realistic testing configurations (waveforms and compaction conditions), elastic recovery and long-term healing potential, as well as the effect of saturation on moisture-damage potential.
outcomes: Field and laboratory analysis of extended-life pavement sections (20+ years of data)
Traffic Speed Deflectometer (TSD) and FWD network-level evaluation
Multiscale mechanical response characterization under realistic conditions
Elastic recovery and long-term healing potential assessment
Updated design procedures for IDOT perpetual pavement guidelines

projects.title: Development of a Pavement Friction Management Program
projects.summary: Project focused on the development of a Pavement Friction Management Program for the Illinois Department of Transportation, contributing to the creation of a statewide friction management system. Part of the research scope involves analyzing aggregate characteristics to be incorporated into the tool, specially in terms of morphology properties and susceptibility to degradation in order to support treatment selection decisions towards a vision-zero perspective of roadway safety. Collaboration with IDOT, UIUC Illinois Center for Transportation, Rutgers University, and Arora & Associates.
outcomes: Aggregate characterization via AIMS (morphology) and Micro-Deval (degradation)
AIMS-based aggregate morphology database for Illinois surface mix sources
Friction thresholds and treatment selection framework
Statewide friction management system supporting vision-zero safety goals
Technical reports and decision-support tool for IDOT

projects.title: Mechanistic-Empirical Design Procedures for Flexible Pavements
projects.summary: Project focused on field and laboratory evaluation of asphalt concrete (AC) mixes for perpetual flexible pavements in light of recent advancements in asphalt technology, mainly the use of recycled materials and modified binders. The project combined multiscale laboratory testing with different modeling strategies, validating the use of a Bayesian neural network as an appropriate predictor of current Illinois' AC mixes modulus for pavement design purposes. Non-destructive approaches, including ultrasonic pulse velocity testing and field modulus measurements, were also explored. Additionally, limiting tensile strain criteria for extended-life performance were examined through four-point bending fatigue protocols.
outcomes: Dynamic modulus master curves for Illinois asphalt concrete mixes
Bayesian neural network model for improved modulus prediction (outperforms traditional algorithms)
Ultrasonic pulse velocity (UPV) non-destructive screening method validation
Four-point bending fatigue characterization for design input calibration
Updated fatigue endurance limit (FEL) criteria for extended-life pavements
Published: Canadian J. Civil Engineering (2024)

projects.title: Development of Porous Asphalt Mixtures for Southern Lake Michigan Flexible Pavements
projects.summary: Project focused on resilience strategies for coastal areas of the Lake Michigan region. Evaluation of the long-term performance in the field of porous asphalt mixtures in the city of Chicago, covering aspects such as surface texture evolution, binder aging, internal structure by means of CT-scanning, and viscoelastic response under freeze–thaw cycles and saturation conditions. Collaboration with IISG, UIUC, Illinois Center for Transportation, Chicago Department of Transportation (CDOT), and S.T.A.T.E. Testing, LLC.
outcomes: Field performance evaluation of porous asphalt from seven Chicago roadway sections
Surface texture evolution and raveling analysis over time
Binder aging assessment and ductility characterization at depth
CT scanning characterization of internal structure and air void distribution
Saturation sensitivity and freeze-thaw durability testing
Design recommendations for cold-region porous asphalt in coastal zones
Published: Case Studies in Construction Materials (2024)

projects.title: 3DTrans Project: Urban Transport Systems (Air Pollution, Road Safety, Social Vulnerability)
projects.client: Federal University of Ceará, Brazil (CNPq Funded)
projects.summary: National research project in Brazil addressing urban transport sustainability by investigating the relationship between urban roads' infrastructure, vehicle emissions, traffic safety, and social impacts. The scope of the project integrates traditional and innovative concepts (such as portable measuring devices for emissions and driving patterns) in infrastructure to the proposition of 'green routes', aligned with UN Agenda 2030 (Goals 9, 11, 13). Multidisciplinary collaboration with 20+ research collaborators worldwide.
outcomes: Investigation of roadway condition (IRI) and vehicle emissions relationships
Assessment of pavement geometry, friction, and surface texture on safety
Analysis of roadway condition impact on vulnerable urban communities
Green route design methodology aligned with UN SDG 9, 11, 13
Sustainable urban transport framework

projects.title: Analysis of Atmospheric Pollutant Concentrations from Asphalt Paving Services
projects.client: Federal University of Ceará, Brazil (FUNCAP Funded)
projects.summary: National research project in Brazil evaluating pollutant emissions from asphalt paving services in urban environments. Focused on diagnosing environmental and public health impacts of pavement construction stages and developing sustainable methodologies to mitigate them. This project consolidated the research area on material characterization and sustainability in pavement engineering of the Graduate Program in Transportation Engineering at the Federal University of Ceará.
outcomes: Environmental and public health impact assessment of paving operations
Pollutant emission characterization from asphalt construction
Sustainable mitigation methodologies for construction stages
Consolidated sustainability research area in transportation engineering

projects.title: Evaluation of Aggregate Shape Properties: Crushing, Mineralogy, and Degradation
projects.client: Federal University of Ceará, Brazil (CNPq Funded)
projects.summary: Project investigating how mineralogy, crushing processes, and degradation affect aggregate morphology. Applied digital image processing (AIMS – Aggregate Image Measurement System) to characterize shape, angularity, and surface texture, supporting improved selection and use of aggregates in infrastructure applications.
outcomes: AIMS-based characterization of aggregate shape, angularity, and surface texture
Assessment of mineralogy effects on aggregate morphology
Evaluation of crushing process impacts on shape properties
Degradation analysis and classification framework
Improved aggregate selection methodology for infrastructure
